mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150k persons signals robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above 1.5% and continues climbing, it signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on future equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 1.5% and shows positive 3-month trend
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.16% in November 2017, below tightening threshold
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 1.5% with positive trend, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with accelerating housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer demand and construction activity, which jointly drive positive equity returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate revenue and capital expenditure cycles.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 492.7B and HOUST at 1290k in October 2017
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 58% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST show month-over-month growth, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month, signaling weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side constraints that compress profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices earnings downward.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances following INDPRO declines > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.015 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as corporate earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% in November 2017, near cycle lows
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0%, creating a negative real rate environment that initially supports equity valuations but signals future tightening pressure, producing mixed but ultimately positive 60-day returns as the market prices in gradual normalization.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 247.6 and FEDFUNDS at 1.16% in November 2017
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 56% of instances meeting both conditions, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter exceeds 2.5% annualized, indicating robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.025 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) remains below 4.5%, signaling a supply-constrained economy where wage pressures and input costs rise faster than productivity, compressing profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.4 and UNRATE at 4.1% in November 2017
regime_hint: tight labor market with supply constraints
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances meeting both conditions, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis.
